summary: Matched and linked the common item foods unit by making `melee/it/items/itfoods.c` a Matching object, correcting a few ABI/type details around food item state setup, and adjusting symbol visibility/local labels for the linked output. The decomp.dev report confirmed `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` reached 100% and the project gained linked code/data, with a small reported percentage regression in still-unmatched `it_2E5A`.

key_files.reason: Main matched unit; renamed the food item state table symbol to `it_803F5DB0`, included `it/inlines.h` locally, changed `getRandMax` to take an `Article*`, removed the padding workaround, and reshaped `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` to match.
Changed `melee/it/items/itfoods.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)`, enabling the unit to link as matched code.
Updated itfoods motion callbacks from local to global symbols and converted several adjacent data/string/float symbols to local `@NNN` labels to match the linked map.
Changed `itFoods_ItemVars` fields `x0` and `heal_amount` from signed `s32` to unsigned `u32`, reflecting actual usage/storage for food index and heal amount.
Corrected `it_80273318` implementation signature by removing an unused third `s32` parameter.
Updated the declaration of `it_80273318` to take only `Item_GObj*` and `HSD_Joint*`.
Updated a caller of `it_80273318` to the corrected two-argument signature.
Removed `it/inlines.h` from the public itfoods header after moving the include into the C file.

what_changed: `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` was rewritten enough to reach 100% match according to the decomp.dev bot report.
`getRandMax` changed from `static s32 getRandMax(Item* ip)` to `inline u32 getRandMax(Article* article)`, using `article->x4_specialAttributes` directly.

`itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` now calls `HSD_Randi(getRandMax(ip->xC4_article_data))`, stores the random index and heal amount, introduces a temporary `temp = rand`, and calls `it_80273318(gobj, attr[temp].x4)`.
The old `PAD_STACK(8)` in `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` was removed, implying the revised expression shape and temporary handled matching without manual padding.
`itFoods_ItemVars` fields for the food item variable block changed from signed to unsigned: `x0` and `heal_amount` are now `u32`.
`it_80273318` was corrected to a two-argument API and its call sites were updated, removing the previously passed third value.
The itfoods state table global in `itfoods.c` was renamed from `it_803F83F0` to `it_803F5DB0`, aligning source with the symbol map/data address shown in the diff.
`it/inlines.h` was moved out of `itfoods.h` and into `itfoods.c`, reducing public-header include exposure.

Symbol-map scope changed for itfoods motion callbacks (`itFoods_UnkMotion{0,1,2,3}_{Anim,Phys,Coll}`) from local to global, while several nearby data literals were demoted from global `it_...` names to local `@NNN` symbols.
smart_moves: Correcting the callee signature of `it_80273318` instead of preserving a bogus unused third argument simplified both the matched itfoods code and an existing caller in `it_2E5A.c`.
Changing `getRandMax` to accept the already-used `Article*` avoided reloading through `Item*` and better matched the code shape around `ip->xC4_article_data`.
The explicit `temp = rand` before indexing `attr[temp].x4` is a small but important expression-shaping tactic for matching compiler register/lifetime behavior.
Removing `PAD_STACK(8)` after restructuring the function shows the match was achieved by improving source fidelity rather than relying on artificial stack padding.
Moving `it/inlines.h` from the public header into the implementation file keeps inline dependencies local to the translation unit that needs them.
Synchronizing symbol visibility/local labels with the linked result helped the object link cleanly after switching the unit to Matching.
decomp_lessons: When a function appears to need an extra argument at call sites, verify the callee body and ABI evidence; the correct match may require deleting a phantom parameter rather than forcing casts.
For item article attribute access, passing the narrower structure pointer (`Article*`) to helper functions can produce a closer match than passing the containing `Item*` and re-deriving the article.
Unsigned item variable fields can matter for matching even when names are still generic; update struct field signedness when usage indicates indexes/counts or nonnegative values.
A temporary local variable can be necessary to preserve the compiler's expected register allocation or reload pattern, especially before array indexing into attributes.
Do not leave inline-only dependencies in public item headers if only the C file needs them; header hygiene can reduce incidental dependencies while preserving matches.
After linking a unit, revisit `symbols.txt` for both function visibility and literal/data labels; matching source alone is not enough if the map still describes stale local/global scopes.
Bot percentage regressions in unrelated unmatched units can occur when shared prototypes change; record them, but do not necessarily treat them as blocking if the target unit links and improves.
naming_conventions: Food logic functions use the item prefix and behavior/state naming pattern, e.g. `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned`, `itFoods_Logic18_PickedUp`, `itFoods_Logic18_Dropped`, and `itFoods_Logic18_EvtUnk`.
Unknown per-state callbacks follow `itFoods_UnkMotionN_Anim`, `itFoods_UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `itFoods_UnkMotionN_Coll` naming.
Unidentified data symbols may be kept as address-based `it_803F...` globals when referenced globally, but compiler-local literals should be represented as `@NNN` local labels in `symbols.txt`.
Item variable structs use item-specific names such as `itFoods_ItemVars`, while uncertain fields remain generic (`x0`) until semantics are known.
The matched state table was represented by its address-derived name `it_803F5DB0` rather than the previous mismatched `it_803F83F0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Switched `itfoods.c` to Matching only after local code and symbol-map changes were enough to link.
Removed manual `PAD_STACK(8)` by changing the source expression structure in `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned`.
Used an `inline` helper returning `u32` for random bound retrieval from `Article->x4_specialAttributes`.
Introduced a local `s32 temp` copy of `rand` before the `it_80273318` joint lookup to shape generated code.
Changed `itFoods_ItemVars` field signedness from `s32` to `u32` to align stores/loads for food index and heal amount.
Corrected `it_80273318` to a two-argument function, which removed casts and dead arguments at call sites.
Adjusted symbol scopes in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`: itfoods motion callbacks became global; several literals/floats became local labels.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code +120 bytes, linked code +1208 bytes, linked data +104 bytes.
Confirmed one new match: `main/melee/it/items/itfoods` item `itFoods_Logic18_Spawned` improved from 93.33% to 100.00%.
Reported one regression in unmatched `main/melee/it/items/it_2E5A`, where `it_802E6380` changed from 58.27% to 58.23% despite 0 byte delta.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `itFoods_ItemVars.x0` beyond the generic field name.
Search for later work on `it_80273318` to see if the function receives a semantic name for setting `Item.xC8_joint`.
Check subsequent PRs touching `it_2E5A.c` and `it_802E6380` to see whether the small unmatched regression from this prototype correction was resolved.
Look for later symbol-map cleanups around `it_803F5DB0`, `ItemStateTable_MsBomb`, and nearby local string/float labels.
Search for other item matching PRs that remove phantom arguments from shared item helper functions.
